documentation.text:
@title  ClaimRegistry
 @notice Manages the lifecycle of LUNA claims and stores the latest
         committed posterior for each claim.
 @dev    Paper-aligned design invariants (Section IV-A):
         1. Three-state machine: None -> Open -> Finalized.
            The Finalized state is absorbing; updatePosterior() and
            finalizeClaim() both revert if the claim is not Open.
         2. Only OracleController may transition claim state or write
            posteriors.  Applications open and finalize claims by
            calling the corresponding OracleController functions,
            which in turn call this registry.  This keeps a single
            authorised entry point for all claim mutations and matches
            the paper's workflow (Section III-E).
         3. Every state transition and posterior update records
            block.number.  The oracle also records the CPTStore block
            height at BN reconstruction time; together these two
            values allow auditors to detect parameter-submission races.
         4. posteriorPPH and posteriorPPR are range-checked
            independently against SCALE.  They are NOT required to
            sum to SCALE because PPH and PPR are marginally independent
            root nodes; mutual exclusivity of claim types
            is enforced at the application layer, not in the BN.
         5. A reverse mapping externalKey -> claimId allows the oracle
            to resolve visit-metadata keys to on-chain identifiers in
            O(1) without scanning event logs.  A duplicate-key guard
            prevents two claims from being opened for the same visit.

@notice Scale factor shared across LUNA contracts.
         All posteriors p in [0,1] are stored as round(p * SCALE).
@notice Deployer; permitted only to bind the OracleController.

@notice The single authorised writer for all claim mutations.
         Set once after deployment via setOracleController().

@notice externalKey => claimId reverse lookup.
         Enables O(1) key-to-id resolution by the off-chain oracle.
         Value 0 means no claim exists for that key (claimId 0 is
         distinguished by checking claims[0].state != None).

@dev    Tracks whether a given externalKey has been registered.
         Needed because claimId 0 is a valid identifier.
@notice Next claimId; incremented atomically on each openClaim().
@notice Emitted when the OracleController binding changes.
@notice Emitted when a claim is finalized.

@notice Returns the full claim record.
 @param  claimId  Internal claim identifier.

devdoc.details: Paper-aligned design invariants (Section IV-A):         1. Three-state machine: None -> Open -> Finalized.            The Finalized state is absorbing; updatePosterior() and            finalizeClaim() both revert if the claim is not Open.         2. Only OracleController may transition claim state or write            posteriors.  Applications open and finalize claims by            calling the corresponding OracleController functions,            which in turn call this registry.  This keeps a single            authorised entry point for all claim mutations and matches            the paper's workflow (Section III-E).         3. Every state transition and posterior update records            block.number.  The oracle also records the CPTStore block            height at BN reconstruction time; together these two            values allow auditors to detect parameter-submission races.         4. posteriorPPH and posteriorPPR are range-checked            independently against SCALE.  They are NOT required to            sum to SCALE because PPH and PPR are marginally independent            root nodes; mutual exclusivity of claim types            is enforced at the application layer, not in the BN.         5. A reverse mapping externalKey -> claimId allows the oracle            to resolve visit-metadata keys to on-chain identifiers in            O(1) without scanning event logs.  A duplicate-key guard            prevents two claims from being opened for the same visit.

finalizeClaim(uint256).details: Finalized is absorbing: this function and updatePosterior()         both revert if called again on a Finalized claim.         Called by OracleController when the application signals         that its completion condition has been met.

openClaim(bytes32).details: Called by OracleController on behalf of an application.         externalKey must be unique; duplicate keys are rejected         to prevent two claims from being created for the same         physician visit.

returns.claimId: Internal on-chain identifier for this claim.

resolveKey(bytes32).details: Returns (claimId, true) if registered,         (0, false) if not found.

returns.found: True iff a claim is registered for this key.

updatePosterior(uint256,uint256,uint256).details: Accepts repeated updates; each replaces the previous         posterior and appends a new audit event.         Reverts if the claim is Finalized (absorbing state).         posteriorPPH and posteriorPPR are checked independently;         they need not sum to SCALE (see contract-level note on         marginal independence of the PPH and PPR root nodes).

_keyRegistered.details: Tracks whether a given externalKey has been registered.         Needed because claimId 0 is a valid identifier.

devdoc.title: ClaimRegistry

ClaimFinalized(uint256,uint256).notice: Emitted when a claim is finalized.

ClaimOpened(uint256,bytes32,uint256).notice: Emitted when a new claim is opened.         Both claimId and externalKey are indexed for efficient         lookup by either identifier.

ClaimPosteriorUpdated(uint256,uint256,ui.notice: Emitted on every posterior update.         blockNumber enables auditors to correlate the update with         the CPTStore parameter snapshot used by the oracle.

OracleControllerUpdated(address,address).notice: Emitted when the OracleController binding changes.

SCALE().notice: Scale factor shared across LUNA contracts.         All posteriors p in [0,1] are stored as round(p * SCALE).

externalKeyToClaimId(bytes32).notice: externalKey => claimId reverse lookup.         Enables O(1) key-to-id resolution by the off-chain oracle.         Value 0 means no claim exists for that key (claimId 0 is         distinguished by checking claims[0].state != None).

finalizeClaim(uint256).notice: Finalizes an open claim.  Transition: Open -> Finalized.

getClaimState(uint256).notice: Returns the lifecycle state of a claim.

nextClaimId().notice: Next claimId; incremented atomically on each openClaim().

openClaim(bytes32).notice: Opens a new claim.  Transition: None -> Open.

oracleController().notice: The single authorised writer for all claim mutations.         Set once after deployment via setOracleController().

owner().notice: Deployer; permitted only to bind the OracleController.

resolveKey(bytes32).notice: Resolves a visit-metadata key to an on-chain claimId.

setOracleController(address).notice: Binds the OracleController address.         Must be called once after deployment, before any claims         are opened.  Can be updated by the owner if the         controller contract is redeployed.

updatePosterior(uint256,uint256,uint256).notice: Updates the committed posterior for an open claim.

userdoc.notice: Manages the lifecycle of LUNA claims and stores the latest         committed posterior for each claim.
